2604.11539.title: CLAY: Conditional Visual Similarity Modulation in Vision-Language Embedding Space
2604.11539.paper_summary: Human perception of visual similarity is inherently adaptive and subjective, depending on the users' interests and focus. However, most image retrieval systems fail to reflect this flexibility, relying on a fixed, monolithic metric that cannot incorporate multiple conditions simultaneously. To address this, we propose CLAY, an adaptive similarity computation method that reframes the embedding space of pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as a text-conditional similarity space without additional training. This design separates the textual conditioning process and visual feature extraction, allowing highly efficient and multi-conditioned retrieval with fixed visual embeddings. We also construct a synthetic evaluation dataset CLAY-EVAL, for comprehensive assessment under diverse conditioned retrieval settings. Experiments on standard datasets and our proposed dataset show that CLAY achieves high retrieval accuracy and notable computational efficiency compared to previous works.
2604.11539.paper_summary_zh: 人類對視覺相似性的感知本質上是適應性和主觀的，取決於使用者的興趣和焦點。<lb/>然而，大多數圖像檢索系統未能反映這種靈活性，依賴於一個固定的、單一的度量，無法同時納入多種條件。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了CLAY，一種自適應相似性計算方法，將預訓練的視覺-語言模型（VLMs）的嵌入空間重新構建為文本條件相似性空間，而無需額外訓練。<lb/>這種設計將文本條件過程和視覺特徵提取分開，允許以固定的視覺嵌入進行高效且多條件的檢索。<lb/>我們還構建了一個合成評估數據集CLAY-EVAL，以便在多樣的條件檢索設置下進行全面評估。<lb/>在標準數據集和我們提出的數據集上的實驗表明，與以往的工作相比，CLAY實現了高檢索準確率和顯著的計算效率。
2604.11539.author: Sohwi Lim et.al.
2604.11539.authors: Sohwi Lim, Lee Hyoseok, Jungjoon Park, Tae-Hyun Oh
